scenario.title: Pre-existing hardware defect with late Plan purchase
scenario.description: Customer reported a malfunctioning camera on their iPhone XS that was present before purchasing the AppleCare+ Plan a month later.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to a pre-existing condition on any Covered iPhone if you purchased the Plan after you purchased the device.

scenario.reasoning: The defect is a pre-existing condition identified prior to purchasing the AppleCare+ Plan, which is explicitly excluded from coverage per section 4.2(viii). As such, no service fee applies because the claim is denied on eligibility grounds.

persona.defining quirks: Has a habit of doodling during meetings or when thinking
persona.detailed job description: Oversees and coordinates the administrative duties in schools and ensures that the office operates efficiently
persona.family presence and age: With related children 5 to 17 years only
persona.household type: Married couple household with children of the householder less than 18
persona.ideology: Believes in the importance of education and continuous learning
persona.industry category: EDU-Other Schools And Instruction, And Educational Support Services
persona.lifestyle: Organized and structured, with a focus on continuous learning and development
persona.mannerisms: Often uses hand gestures when explaining something
persona.occupation category: OFF-First-Line Supervisors Of Office And Administrative Support Workers
persona.personal time: Reading or planning for the next work day
persona.political views: Democrat, with a focus on education policies

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario description:
Customer reported a malfunctioning camera on their iPhone XS that was present before purchasing the AppleCare+ Plan a month later.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):
{
  "device": "iPhone XS (256 GB)",
  "plan_agreement_number": "APLCARE-8723649110",
  "serial_number": "DX4HV5K9MF7R",
  "phone_number": "+1-415-555-1234"
}

Persona to role-play as:
{

  "ancestry": "Irish",

  },
  "citizenship": "Born in the United States",
  "class of worker": "Employee of a private for-profit company or business, or of an individual, for wages, salary, or commissions",
  "defining quirks": "Has a habit of doodling during meetings or when thinking",
  "detailed job description": "Oversees and coordinates the administrative duties in schools and ensures that the office operates efficiently",
  "disability": "None",
  "education": "Associate's degree",
  "employment status": "Civilian employed, at work",
  "family presence and age": "With related children 5 to 17 years only",
  "health insurance": "With health insurance coverage",
  "household language": "English",
  "household type": "Married couple household with children of the householder less than 18",
  "ideology": "Believes in the importance of education and continuous learning",
  "income": "65000",
  "industry category": "EDU-Other Schools And Instruction, And Educational Support Services",
  "lifestyle": "Organized and structured, with a focus on continuous learning and development",
  "mannerisms": "Often uses hand gestures when explaining something",
  "marital status": "Never married",
  "occupation category": "OFF-First-Line Supervisors Of Office And Administrative Support Workers",
  "personal time": "Reading or planning for the next work day",

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hi, I need to file a claim for my iPhone XS. The camera stopped working, and I think it was like that even before I got the AppleCare+ plan a month ago.
messages.raw_text: Hi, I need to file a claim for my iPhone XS. The camera stopped working, and I think it was like that even before I got the AppleCare+ plan a month ago.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone XS to start the claim process?
messages.raw_text: Could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone XS to start the claim process?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure, the Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-8723649110 and the serial number is DX4HV5K9MF7R.
messages.raw_text: Sure, the Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-8723649110 and the serial number is DX4HV5K9MF7R.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you. Can you describe the issue with your iPhone's camera in more detail? For example, is it not focusing, not opening, or showing errors?
messages.raw_text: Thank you. Can you describe the issue with your iPhone's camera in more detail? For example, is it not focusing, not opening, or showing errors?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yeah, the camera just doesn’t focus properly anymore. It’s been like that since before I got the plan, so I don’t know if it’s a defect or what. It’s pretty annoying, and I want to get it checked out.
messages.raw_text: Yeah, the camera just doesn’t focus properly anymore. It’s been like that since before I got the plan, so I don’t know if it’s a defect or what. It’s pretty annoying, and I want to get it checked out.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the details. Since the issue started before your plan purchase, it may be considered a defect or an excluded cause. Can you confirm if you've already tried any troubleshooting steps, like restarting the device or updating iOS?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the details. Since the issue started before your plan purchase, it may be considered a defect or an excluded cause. Can you confirm if you've already tried any troubleshooting steps, like restarting the device or updating iOS?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yeah, I’ve already tried restarting it, and I keep my software up-to-date. Nothing has helped though. It just doesn’t focus properly. I just wanna get it fixed or replaced.
messages.raw_text: Yeah, I’ve already tried restarting it, and I keep my software up-to-date. Nothing has helped though. It just doesn’t focus properly. I just wanna get it fixed or replaced.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Understood. Do you see any error messages or signs of physical damage, like cracks or liquid exposure, that could be related to the camera issue?
messages.raw_text: Understood. Do you see any error messages or signs of physical damage, like cracks or liquid exposure, that could be related to the camera issue?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Nope, no cracks, no liquids, and no error messages. It all looks fine visually, but the camera just won’t focus right. It’s been like that for a while honestly.
messages.raw_text: Nope, no cracks, no liquids, and no error messages. It all looks fine visually, but the camera just won’t focus right. It’s been like that for a while honestly.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the information. Based on what you've described, it sounds like a possible hardware defect. This claim will be processed accordingly. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we proceed?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the information. Based on what you've described, it sounds like a possible hardware defect. This claim will be processed accordingly. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we proceed?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that’s all. Just want to get it sorted. Thanks.
messages.raw_text: No, that’s all. Just want to get it sorted. Thanks.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: You're welcome. Your claim will now be processed. You will get notified as soon as it is completed. If you need any further assistance, feel free to contact us. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: You're welcome. Your claim will now be processed. You will get notified as soon as it is completed. If you need any further assistance, feel free to contact us. Have a great day!

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Thanks, I appreciate it. Hopefully, this gets sorted out quickly. Have a good one.
messages.raw_text: Thanks, I appreciate it. Hopefully, this gets sorted out quickly. Have a good one.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you! We appreciate your patience. If you need anything else in the future, don't hesitate to reach out. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: Thank you! We appreciate your patience. If you need anything else in the future, don't hesitate to reach out. Have a great day! <END>
